data.title: OCBC Group CEO: Expect 2025 Net Interest Margin to Be Between 1.90% and 1.95%
OCBC Group CEO: Expect 2025 Net Interest Income to Fall by Mid-Single-Digit Percentage
Indonesian's exports up 11.29% y/y in June
data.description:
Indonesia's exports in June rose 11.29% on a yearly basis to $23.44 billion, data from the country's statistics bureau showed on Friday, higher than the 10.41% forecast by economists polled by Reuters.

Indonesian exporters in recent months have brought forward their shipments to the United States ahead of the latest deadline set by President Donald Trump for tariff negotiations.

Indonesia emerged as one of the earliest countries to strike a deal with Washington to lower the U.S. tariff to 19%, from a previously threatened level of 32%, ahead of the Aug. 1 deadline.

In the negotiations, Jakarta agreed to eliminate most tariffs affecting U.S. industrial and agricultural products and to buy more American goods.

U.S. President Donald Trump issued an executive order saying the new tariff rates will be implemented in seven days.

Southeast Asia's largest economy booked a $4.11 billion surplus in June, higher than the poll's forecast of $3.45 billion but down slightly from $4.30 billion in May.

June imports were worth $19.33 billion, up 4.28% from a year earlier.

data.title: Reddit's stock is soaring after earnings. This is why.
data.description:
By James Rogers

The social-media platform's second-quarter revenue and earnings blow past Wall Street's expectations

Shares of Reddit Inc. jumped about 18% in extended trading Thursday after the social-media platform's second-quarter results blew past Wall Street's expectations for revenue and earnings, boosted by surging ad revenue and robust user growth.

Reddit's (RDDT) daily active users topped Wall Street's expectations for the second straight quarter, growing 21% on a year-over-year basis to 110.4 million. Analysts surveyed by FactSet were looking for 109.02 million daily active users.

International daily active users rose 32% to 60.1 million, while U.S. daily active users rose 11% to 50.3 million. Reddit's CFO Drew Vollero told MarketWatch that the company's Machine Translation, or translating content for global audiences, has been extended to 23 languages, up from 13 last quarter.

"We're a young company, so we're adding resources outside the U.S.," he said, highlighting the company's overseas marketing efforts. "We're starting to do partnerships with athletes and organizations outside the U.S.," he added.

San Francisco-based Reddit defines a daily active unique user as one the company can identify with a unique identifier and who has visited a page on the Reddit website or opened a Reddit application at least once over a 24-hour period.

Reddit's quarterly profit was its fourth on a GAAP basis since the company went public in March 2024. The company reported net income of $89 million, or 45 cents a share, after a net loss of $10 million, or a loss of 6 cents a share, in the prior year's quarter. Analysts surveyed by FactSet were expecting earnings of 19 cents a share. Reddit's net income was 18% of revenue.

The company's revenue rose 78% to $500 million, well above the FactSet consensus estimate of $426 million. Ad revenue rose 84% to $465 million.

"The momentum on the commercial business went up a notch," Vollero told MarketWatch, noting that the company's advertiser count was up 50% year over year, marking the highest growth in three years.

The CFO added that revenue in nine of Reddit's 15 top advertising channels was up 75% or more. "I think that the advertising market is better than it has been in the last couple of years," he said, and cited particular strength in the tech, financial and retail verticals, as well as pharma and medical.

Reddit also gave a robust outlook. For the third quarter, the company expects revenue between $535 million and $545 million, above the FactSet consensus estimate of $473 million. Reddit also expects adjusted Ebitda in the range of $185 million to $195 million.

The gross margin was 90.8%, up from 89.5% in the prior year's quarter.

In May, Seaport Research Partners upgraded Reddit to buy from neutral, saying that fears about the impact of changes to Google's search engine were overblown.

Speaking during the conference call to discuss the results, Reddit Chief Executive Steve Huffman said that the company's core search product now has 70 million weekly users. Reddit Answers, an AI-powered feature designed to make it easier for users to find what they are looking for, has also grown to 6 million weekly users, up from 1 million last quarter. "We're expanding Reddit Answers globally, integrating it more deeply into the core search experience and making search a central feature across Reddit," he added, according to a CallStreet transcript.

Reddit recently joined the Russell 3000 and 1000 indexes, and there has been speculation that it could become an addition to the S&P 500 index SPX.

"That's outside of our control," Vollero told MarketWatch. "We will just continue to run our business and let them make the decision on who they include."

Somewhat surprisingly, Huffman name-checked British singer Rick Astley during the company's conference call. The Reddit CEO explained that he had met Astley, famous for his 1987 hit "Never Gonna Give You Up," and the "Rickroll" phenomenon, at the recent Cannes Lions festival. Huffman described the Rickroll as one of the first memes on Reddit. "I was just really interested in hearing how grateful he was for the second act of his career, being a part of that," he said.

Reddit shares ended Thursday's session up 7.5%. The stock is up almost 250% since the company's IPO last year.

-James Rogers

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Malaysia's Five-Year Growth Target Looks Achievable — Market Talk
data.description:
0221 GMT - Malaysia's economic plan targeting 4.5%-5.5% GDP growth annually from 2026 to 2030 appears achievable given recent strong expansion driven by private consumption, CIMB Securities analysts say. But the government's move to cut reliance on foreign workers could raise costs for labor-intensive sectors, Ivy Ng Lee Fang and Lim Yue Jia write in a note. The construction sector stands to benefit from development spending, while affordable housing and clean energy are key focus areas, they add. As Malaysia boosts its role in global semiconductor supply chains, CIMB sees local beneficiaries in IC design and automated test equipment, especially those aligned with AI and advanced packaging. Labor shortages could weigh on segments such as electronics manufacturing services and outsourced semiconductor assembly and testing. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0218 GMT - China's property sales are likely to maintain low levels in August, according to Citi analysts in a research note. The country's July property sales came in worse than expected, an on-month decline of 39% for the 37 listed names, Citi notes. The drop in new home sales is uniform across all city tiers due to seasonally less supply after accelerated launches in June, they say. That said, upgrade demand or luxury home sales at core city centers still outperform the market with scarcity value. With little mention of property in July's Politburo meeting, the absence of new stimulus is a miss to previous investor expectations, Citi notes. August sales could experience a roughly 20% on-year decline, before more supply is observed in 4Q. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0108 GMT - Malaysia is expected to weather new U.S. tariffs with GDP growth likely holding at 4.0% in 2025, says Hong Leong IB analyst Felicia Ling in a note. Despite a 19% U.S. tariff on Malaysian goods from next week and an added 40% duty on transhipped items, key sectors like semiconductors remain exempt for now, she notes. Risks remain if semiconductor exports are later included under trade restrictions, she warns. However, Malaysia's strong electronics ecosystem, global investor interest, and improved global competitiveness should help cushion the impact. Ling expects resilient domestic demand and continued investment momentum to support growth. Given global uncertainties and evolving trade dynamics, Hong Leong expects Bank Negara's policy rate to remain at 2.75% through 2025. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0041 GMT - The yen consolidates against other G-10 and Asian currencies in early trade, but may strengthen on a possible technical recovery. The yen was the standout mover overnight, NAB's Ray Attrill says in commentary. Attrill cites BOJ Gov. Ueda as saying on Thursday that "Right now, the risk of falling behind the curve is not that high" and that the "yen isn't deviating a lot from the BOJ's view." These remarks were sufficient to lift USD/JPY from below 149.00 through 149.50 and then, during European and New York sessions, to a four-month high 150.84, the head of FX Research adds. USD/JPY is 0.1% lower at 150.65; AUD/JPY is steady at 96.86. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0022 GMT - JGBs edge higher amid possible position adjustments following the Bank of Japan's decision and Gov. Ueda's press conference on Thursday. "Ueda indicated that the likelihood of achieving the inflation outlook has improved compared to the previous meeting," two members of JPMorgan's Japan Markets Research say in a note. "However, he did not indicate the timing of the next hike," the members say. Amid a lack of signs of an imminent global/U..S. recession or unexpected political developments, short-term yields are likely to stay mostly range-bound, the members add. The 10-year JGB yield is down 0.5 bp at 1.545%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0016 GMT - Japanese stocks are lower in early trade as caution over earnings and domestic politics continue. Chip and bank stocks are leading the declines. Lasertec is down 5.6% and Resona Holdings is 1.6% lower. Meanwhile, a weaker yen is offering some support for the market. USD/JPY is at 150.85, compared with 148.75 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching quarterly corporate results as well as any domestic political developments. Mitsubishi Chemical Group and Hoya Corp. are due to announce their earnings later in the day. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 0.5% at 40867.71. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2345 GMT - Japanese stocks may decline as caution over earnings and domestic politics continues. Nikkei futures are down 1.0% at 40770 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 150.80, compared with 148.75 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on quarterly corporate results as well as any domestic political developments. Mitsubishi Chemical Group and Hoya Corp. are scheduled to report their earnings later in the day. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 1.0% to 41069.82 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1957 GMT - Weak labor demand per Canada's payroll data suggest that Statistics Canada's official employment report for June overstated the strength of the job market, National Bank Financial strategists say. Statistics Canada's main employment report is the Labour Force Survey, and for June it reported an 83,100 job gain and a reduction in the jobless rate. The data agency's nonfarm payrolls data are not as timely, with May figures issued Thursday. The payroll report shows hiring in May and April but at a slower pace, as well as a 4.1% drop in job vacancies — falling to the lowest level since 2017. Labor demand "is inconsistent" with June's hiring surge, NBF concludes. "The more data we gather, the more it looks like June's Labour Force ... overstated the strength of the job market." (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

1938 GMT - Treasury yields rise for the month as markets grow more concerned about the impact of tariffs and the Fed looks increasingly unlikely to cut interest rates soon. Economists surveyed by WSJ expect July's jobs report tomorrow to print 100,000, down from June's 147,000, with unemployment rising to 4.2% from 4.1%. August payrolls will also be published before the next Fed meeting. Markets are pricing another hold, according to CME. Inflation is expected to remain sticky. Nuveen forecasts July core CPI, due August 12, at 2.9%. The 10-year rises 0.133 percentage point in July, to 4.360%. The two-year adds 0.230 p.p. to 3.952%.(paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1915 GMT - Blue Owl Capital raised $12.1 billion during 2Q, more than double the $5.4 billion collected in the year-earlier period and a quarterly record for the real-estate and credit investment firm. This year's total comprises $5.8 billion from credit funds and another $5.8 billion from real-assets strategies, mostly real estate, according to a presentation of the firm's results in 2Q. "We saw resilience across our investor base despite fairly disruptive capital markets during the quarter, which speaks to the continued and secular demand for the strategies we offer and the strength of our private-wealth channel," CEO Marc Lipschultz says during an earnings call. Strategies dedicated to wealthy individuals represented roughly 36%, or $4.4 billion, of the latest quarter fundraising, while pools backed by institutional investors accounted for the remaining 64%. (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

1818 GMT - Short-term bond investors seem to be in a win-win position, Nuveen's Anders Persson says. Their securities are set to go up in value when the Fed starts to cut interest rates, which is likely to happen later this year, he says. Moreover, yields are quite attractive by historical standards. Persson says, however, that clients are worried the Fed could lose independence and it would hurt fixed-income investors. But he expects the central bank's status to be preserved. "We have not changed any investment...based on that topic," he says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1816 GMT - Interest rates could remain high for a while, so fixed-income investors need to look across asset classes to ensure no opportunity to pick up extra yield goes untapped, Nuveen's Anders Persson says. "We're really looking to diversify as much as we can." His team is looking beyond Treasurys into areas like securitized debt, preferred stocks and munis. They are also looking overseas. "We've been finding really interesting opportunities in European credit markets," Persson says. Emerging markets are another option as the global economic outlook shifts with President Trump's trade policy, he says. Persson tries to stay away from extended duration, given the long-term uncertainties. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Iron ore heads for weekly loss as hopes fade for China stimulus
data.description:
Iron ore futures prices were little changed on Friday but were set for a weekly loss, as expectations of more stimulus from top consumer China for the struggling property sector faded, dimming demand prospects for the steelmaking ingredient.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) was little changed at 784 yuan a metric ton, as of 0140 GMT, and down 2% so far this week.

The benchmark September iron ore <SZZFU5> on the Singapore Exchange climbed 0.64% to $100.4 a ton, as of 0130 GMT. It has slipped 2.8% so far this week.

The long-awaited China's July Politburo meeting that sets the economic course for the rest of the year failed to unleash stimulus for the embattled property sector, which remains a headwind for the consumption of industrial materials such as steel.

"The meeting sets a positive tone for the current economic situation, which reduced the urgency of rolling out more stimulus policies," said Zhuo Guiqiu, an analyst at broker Jinrui Futures.

Concerns over demand prospects also emerged after China's purchasing managers' index (PMI) fell to the lowest since April at 49.3 in July, missing a median forecast of 49.7 in a Reuters poll and down from 49.7 in June, underscoring weakening demand at home and abroad.

Weighing on prices of the key steelmaking ingredient is also falling demand, Jinrui Futures' Zhuo said.

Average daily hot metal output slid by 0.6% from the prior week to 2.41 million tons in the week as of July 31, the lowest level in three weeks, data from consultancy Mysteel showed.

Hot metal output is typically used to gauge iron ore demand.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE languished, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) down 4.72% and 0.18%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange were mixed. Rebar lost 0.68%, hot-rolled coil and wire rod (SWRcv1) were little changed while stainless steel added 0.43%.

data.title: A Bold New Chapter Begins at CEO SUITE
data.description:
HONG KONG, Aug 1, 2025 - (ACN Newswire) - CEO SUITE, Asia's leading premium workspace provider, is pleased to announce the appointment of Paul MacAndrew as its new Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately.

With over 20 years of leadership experience across Asia Pacific, Europe, and the UK, Paul brings a proven record of business transformation, strategic growth, and customer-driven innovation. Most recently, he led 136 locations across 8 countries as Regional Senior Vice President, Asia Pacific at a global flexible workspace brand-achieving consistent double-digit growth.

As CEO, Paul will guide CEO SUITE into its next chapter of innovation, agility, and global expansion beyond Asia, reinforcing the company's position as the trusted business hub for modern enterprises.

"We are excited to welcome Paul to the CEO SUITE family!" said Ms. Mee Kim, President and Founder of CEO SUITE. "Under his leadership, we are launching an AI-powered workspace built for the MZ digital generation. This marks a bold new phase of smarter workspaces, faster growth, and broader global presence."

Founded in 1997 by Ms. Mee Kim, a pioneer in the coworking industry with over 36 years of experience, CEO SUITE is the only coworking company powered by a team of over 200 top industry professionals, many of whom have been with the company for over 10 to 25 years. This rare depth of expertise and loyalty ensures exceptional service, long-term stability, and unwavering commitment to client success.

Now in its 28th year, CEO SUITE operates 21 locations across 11 major Asian cities, continuing to shape the future of work-powered by technology, human connection, and professional excellence.

www.ceosuite.com

Source: CEO SUITE Limited

Copyright 2025 ACN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: Power Racks Seen as Growth Driver for Delta Electronics — Market Talk
data.description: Delta Electronics is likely riding the increasing migration by companies to power racks, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. More components such as back-up battery units manufactured by Delta Electronics are adopted in high-voltage power racks, the analysts say. The migration to power racks is likely to be key growth driver for the electronics manufacturer's revenue over the next few years. Daiwa forecasts the company's revenue from artificial-intelligence servers' power supply units to grow by 68% to NT$67 billion in 2026. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to NT$688.00 from NT$475.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 4.1% higher at NT$590.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0224 GMT - Delta Electronics is likely riding the increasing migration by companies to power racks, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. More components such as back-up battery units manufactured by Delta Electronics are adopted in high-voltage power racks, the analysts say. The migration to power racks is likely to be key growth driver for the electronics manufacturer's revenue over the next few years. Daiwa forecasts the company's revenue from artificial-intelligence servers' power supply units to grow by 68% to NT$67 billion in 2026. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to NT$688.00 from NT$475.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 4.1% higher at NT$590.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0158 GMT - HD Hyundai Heavy Industries could gain from a recovery in U.S. demand for liquefied natural gas carriers likely in late 2H of 2025 or early 1H of 2026, Daiwa Capital analysts Mike Oh and Daeho Son say. Final investment decisions on U.S. LNG projects are likely to proceed without major disruptions, the analysts write in a note. They raise their EPS forecasts for the South Korean shipbuilder by 29% each for 2025 and 2026 to reflect its backlog order mix that is improving at a faster-than-expected pace. The company's new contract wins so far this year have come in at $5.5 billion, about 70% of its $7.7 billion annual target, they note. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0127 GMT - Origin Energy's near-term spending will probably be larger when its part-owned Reedy Creek and Iron Bark gas projects are approved, according to Macquarie analysts. They flag that costs will be structurally higher in the coming three years as the Australia Pacific LNG venture starts replacing production. They tell clients in a note that they anticipate negative cash-flow impacts from fiscal 2027 onwards. Macquarie keeps a neutral rating and A$10.94 target price on the stock, which is up 0.5% at A$11.725. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0014 GMT - Oil edges higher in the early Asian session, supported by supply-disruption concerns. President Trump has given Russian President Putin an Aug. 8 deadline to reach a cease-fire with Ukraine, shortening a 50-day deadline he had set. The deadline for Russia to end its invasion and a full ceasefire has aided some worries over supply disruptions, says Janiv Shah, vice president at Rystad Energy, in an email. Front-month WTI crude oil futures are up 0.1% at $69.33/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are 0.1% higher at $71.80/bbl. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2252 GMT - Beach Energy's quarterly update had a sting in its tail for Euroz Hartleys. While Beach achieved FY 2025 guidance, it also signaled several negative developments. Among them: Faster-than-anticipated field decline in the Otway Basin, prolonged flooding impacts in the Cooper Basin, and a delay to first gas from the second stage of its Waitsia project. Euroz Hartleys cuts a forecast for annual production to 19.7 million BOE, from 23.0 million BOE. It also pares expectations for Ebitda, operating cash flow and dividends. Euroz Hartleys lowers its price target by 13%, to A$1.32/share, noting that drilling of the Beharra Springs Deep 3 gas well suggests the field in the Perth Basin has lower accessible reserves than previously thought. Beach fell 9.3% yesterday, to A$1.17. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1929 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures settle higher in a choppy session. A pickup in LNG feedgas demand and a still "quite hot" second week of August likely contributed to gains, NatGasWeather.com says in a note. "It could also simply be about bulls defending $3," the forecaster adds. The EIA reported a 48 Bcf storage build for last week, which was double the five-year average for the week, increasing the surplus to 195 Bcffrom 171 Bcf. Nymex natural gas settles up 2% at $3.106/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1928 GMT - Oil futures snap a three-day winning streak with the approach of the U.S. tariff deadline and the OPEC+ meeting that's expected to bring another large output increase for September. A rash of U.S. trade deals, and threats of Russia sanctions have oil prices on track for solid weekly gains. If the sanctions are avoided, "if it's business as usual, the fundamentals for oil should weaken," says Neil Crosby, of Sparta Commodities. "A lot of people are expecting weaker demand going forward, OPEC are due to export probably a lot more oil after summer, and with those two things combined you should start to see more of these inventory builds." WTI settles down 1.1% at $69.26 a barrel. September Brent goes off the board at $72.53, down 1%. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1700 GMT - Research from North Dakota State University, done in partnership with the Department of Agriculture's Agricultural Marketing Service, shows that ports globally have adequate capacity for shipping wheat exports, but the ports of leading wheat exporters Russia and Ukraine have, at times, only limited shipping capacity depending on the time of year. But should Russia invest more into its shipping infrastructure, that could dramatically impact the amount of wheat that ships out of the U.S., says the study. "Expanding Russian port infrastructure in the Baltic and Caspian Sea regions results in a reduction of U.S. exports of between 550,000 to 875,000 metric tons per year, depending on the degree of expansion," the study says. CBOT wheat is down 0.9%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1629 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures edge up after the weekly storage build of 48 Bcf is above average and at the high end of analyst estimates. "While the market digests the bearish headline, fresh demand signals are emerging overseas," Gelber & Associates says in a note. South Korea's pledge to buy around $100 billion of U.S. LNG and other energy products over four years is "equivalent to roughly 3.5 Bcf/d of incremental call on U.S. gas when fully phased in," the firm says. The trade deal underscores "the widening geopolitical safety net under long-term American LNG projects, even as near-term fundamentals remain loose." Nymex natural gas is up 0.6% at $3.062/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1512 GMT - Shell is using its operational and financial strength to weather challenging macroeconomic conditions, Quilter Cheviot's Maurizio Carulli writes. While earnings in its key liquefied natural gas division dropped 30%, it won't be too much of a concern for investors, Carulli writes. The unit will bounce back once prices begin to recover, Carulli adds. Shares trade up 1.6% at 2,722 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

1452 GMT - U.S. natural gas inventories increased by 48 billion cubic feet last week to 3,123 Bcf, increasing the surplus over the five-year average to 195 Bcf from 171 Bcf the week before, the EIA reports. The storage increase was bigger than the 24 Bcf average for the week, and above the 36 Bcf estimate in a WSJ survey of analysts. It also narrowed the deficit against the year-earlier level to 123 Bcf from 153 Bcf. Nymex natural gas futures are off 1.1% at $3.012/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1337 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures are hovering around $3 with cooler weather through early next week and continued weakness in LNG feedgas flows weighing. Buoyant production and ample inventories have also been thwarting any attempted rebounds. EIA storage data due at 10:30 a.m. ET are expected to show a 36 Bcf injection, according to a WSJ survey of analysts, which would increase the surplus over the five-year average to 183 Bcf from 171 Bcf the week before. Nymex natural gas is down 1.7% at $2.992/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Australian Telcos Could Beat Expense Expectations — Market Talk
data.description: Australian telecommunications providers' near-term operating expenses could be lower than expected as they cut costs in customer support and lay off more staff, Macquarie analysts say. They wouldn't be surprised if Telstra makes more job cuts on top of its 550 recent redundancies, listing lay-offs as one of four potential catalysts for the stock. Mobile price rises, M&A activity and capital management are the other things they are looking out for ahead of Telstra's annual result announcement later this month. Macquarie has an outperform rating and A$5.19 target price on Telstra shares, which are down 0.5% at A$4.945. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: ResMed's Australia-listed shares hit record high on strong earnings
data.description:
** ResMed's Australia-listed shares rise as much as 3.9% to a record high of A$44.120

** Stock posts its biggest intraday pct gain since May 21

** Medical device maker posts fourth-quarter profit ahead of Wall Street estimates

** Says profit driven by strong demand for its devices used to manage sleep apnea

** Co posts adjusted profit of $2.55 per share for the quarter ended June 30, compared with estimates of $2.48 per share

** Stock up nearly 3% this week, on track for a third consecutive week of gains

** Stock up 19.2% this year, including the day's move

data.title: OCBC Group CEO: Remain Positive on Regional Growth
China's Property Sales Likely to Maintain Low Levels in August — Market Talk
data.description: China's property sales are likely to maintain low levels in August, according to Citi analysts in a research note. The country's July property sales came in worse than expected, an on-month decline of 39% for the 37 listed names, Citi notes. The drop in new home sales is uniform across all city tiers due to seasonally less supply after accelerated launches in June, they say. That said, upgrade demand or luxury home sales at core city centers still outperform the market with scarcity value. With little mention of property in July's Politburo meeting, the absence of new stimulus is a miss to previous investor expectations, Citi notes. August sales could experience a roughly 20% on-year decline, before more supply is observed in 4Q. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Indonesia Imports Rise Less than Expected
data.description:
Indonesia's imports rose 4.28% yoy to USD 19.33 billion in June 2025, following a 4.14% growth in the previous month and missing market consensus of 6.5%.

Still, it marked the fifth straight month of increase.

data.title: HCM III Acquisition: Units to Start Trading Aug. 1, 2025, Under Ticker Symbol "HCMAU" >HCMA
HCM III Acquisition: Units to List on Nasdaq Global Market >HCMA
HCM III Acquisition Prices IPO of 22M Units at $10 Each >HCMA
China's Manufacturing Activity Tumbles Into Contraction — Update
data.description:
A private gauge of China's manufacturing activity returned to contractionary territory in July, as softening new business growth led factories to scale back production.

The S&P Global China general manufacturing purchasing managers index declined to 49.5 in July from 50.4 in June, according to data released Friday by S&P Global. A reading above 50 suggests an expansion in activity, while a reading below suggests contraction.

Companies lowered selling prices in July amid intensifying competition despite rising input prices, while factors including falling production and stable backlogs prompted Chinese manufacturers to trim headcount in July as new export orders contracted at a faster pace, according to S&P Global.

"While successful business development efforts within the domestic market were able to sustain higher new work inflows, overall sales growth was only fractional as demand from overseas remained subdued on the back of global trade uncertainty," said Jingyi Pan, economics associate director at S&P Global Market Intelligence.

Friday's reading is in line with a competing official gauge, which showed deterioration in factory activity last month. The official manufacturing PMI for last month came in at 49.3, down from June's 49.7.

Write to Singapore Editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Newlox Gold Increases Private Placement to $3 Million
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

    July 31, 2025 – TheNewswire - Vancouver,British Columbia – Newlox Gold Ventures Corp. (the “Corporation”) is pleased toannounce that, further to its press release of July 22, 2025, theCorporation has increased the size of its previously announced privateplacement from 28,571,429 units to 42,857,143 units (“Units”) at a price of $0.07 fortotal gross proceeds of $3 million (the “Private Placement”).

    Each Unit shall consist of one (1) common share (“Share”) in the capital of theCorporation and one (1) common share purchase warrant (“Warrant”). Each Warrant willentitle the holder thereof to acquire one (1) Share of the Corporation(each a “Warrant Share”)at a price of $0.12 per Warrant Share for a period of 36 monthsfollowing the date of issuance.

    The proceeds from the Private Placement will be used to fund theCorporation’s current operations in Costa Rica and for generalworking capital.

    The Private Placement is subject to all necessary regulatoryapprovals. The securities being issued in the private placement willbe subject to a four-month hold period in accordance with applicableCanadian securities laws.

    The securities to be offered pursuant to the Private Placement havenot been, and will not be, registered under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, asamended (the “U.S. SecuritiesAct”) or any U.S. state securities laws, and may not beoffered or sold in the United States absent registration under theU.S. Securities Act and all applicable U.S. state securities laws orcompliance with the requirements of exemptions therefrom. Under Rule144 under the Securities Act, such securities may be resold followinga holding period of six months, subject to certain volume and mannerof sale limitations in the event the holder is deemed an affiliate,and subject to otherwise meeting all applicable conditions of suchrule.  Hedging transactions involving the securities may not beconducted unless in compliance with the Securities Act.

    This news release shall not constitute an offer to sell or thesolicitation of an offer to buy securities in the United States, norshall there be any sale of these securities in any jurisdiction inwhich such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful. The Sharesand Warrants issued under the Private Placement are also subject to aCanadian statutory hold period of four months and one day followingthe closing date.

    About Newlox GoldVentures Corp.

    Newlox Gold Ventures Corp. is an emerging preciousmetals producer dedicated to the recovery of gold and silver fromartisanal and small-scale mining operations across Latin America. TheCompany leverages technology to recover precious metals whileremediating historical mine waste and contributing to local economicdevelopment.

    On Behalf of the Board, Newlox Gold VenturesCorp.

data.title: Indonesian's exports up 11.29% y/y in June
data.description:
Indonesia's exports in June rose 11.29% on a yearly basis to $23.44 billion, data from the country's statistics bureau showed on Friday, higher than the 10.41% forecast by economists polled by Reuters.

The bureau is due to release import and trade balance as well as July inflation data later on Friday.

data.title: Indonesia's palm oil exports at 11 million metric tons in first half
data.description:
Indonesia exported 11 million metric tons of crude and refined palm oil from January to June, the statistics bureau said on Friday.

The bureau's data excludes palm kernel oil, oleochemicals and biodiesel. Indonesia's palm oil association GAPKI usually releases its own data at a later date, which cover more products and so has different export figures.

data.title: US CDC cuts experts out of panels that develop vaccine policy, Bloomberg reports
data.description: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention told physician groups, public health professionals and infectious disease experts that they will no longer be invited to help review vaccine data and develop recommendations, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday.

data.title: KOSPI Slides on Sector Losses, Profit-Taking
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI plunged 2.88% to around 3,150 on Friday, pulling back from multi-year highs as broad-based losses across all sectors and profit-taking by investors weighed on sentiment following the first day of tariff imposition.

Significant drags came from Poongsan Corporation (-15.36%), Doosan Enerbility (-6.25%), SK Hynix (-6.03%), and KB Financial Group (-4.06%).

Adding to the downward pressure, South Korea's factory activity contracted for the sixth consecutive month in July, with the S&P Global Manufacturing PMI falling to 48.0 from 48.7 in June as both output and new orders fell at a sharper pace.

On the flipside, South Korea's exports rose for the second straight month in July, as shipments climbed 5.9% year-on-year to $60.82 billion, while semiconductor exports jumped 39.3%, car shipments rose 8.8%, and ship exports surged 107.6%.

Meanwhile, imports edged up 0.7% to $54.21 billion, narrowing the trade surplus to $6.61 billion from June's $9.08 billion.

data.title: Vietnam domestic prices edge higher on supply concerns; Indonesian premium narrows
data.description:
Domestic coffee prices in Vietnam rose slightly on Friday on supply concerns, as global prices recovered from last week's 16-month low.

Farmers in the Central Highlands, Vietnam's largest coffee-growing region, sold beans (COFVN-DAK) at 99,500-99,700 dong ($3.80-$3.81) per kg, up from last week's 95,700-96,200 dong.

Traders say concerns about a possible dry weather spell in the region could have given domestic prices a boost.

"Farmers had refrained from selling their beans, waiting for more gains in price, which, if it surpasses 100,000 dong per kg, could trigger an increase in supplies," a trader based in Dak Lak province said.

Robusta coffee last traded at $3,331 a metric ton, up from a 16-month low of $3,166 last week.

Traders said they are cautious as they wait for the Trump administration's final decision on the U.S.'s tariff on coffee from top producer Brazil.

In Indonesia, Sumatra Robusta coffee beans for September/October contract were offered at $140-150 premium, a decline from $160 premium last week.

Another trader said the beans were offered at $100 premium for November contract, compared with a $150 premium last week.

A coffee farmer there said harvest is ongoing, while some plantations are currently in the first fruiting stage.

($1 = 26,180 dong)
